title: Podman complaining about missing global policy.json while a local one is present
body:
### Issue Description

I installed Podman through Nix. I have a `policy.json` defined as this in `/home/MYUSER/.config/containers/policy.json`:

```json
{
    "default": [
        {
            "type": "insecureAcceptAnything"
        }
    ]
}
```

 I don't have an `/etc/containers/policy.json`. 

If i issue a `podman image trust show` I get an `Error: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory` . I checked running strace and I do see that `newfstatat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/MYUSER/.config/containers/policy.json", {st_mode=S_IFREG|0644, st_size=93, ...}, 0) = 0` but still get the error of the missing /etc/containers/policy.json file.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Have a local (/home/MYUSER/.config/containers/policy.json) policy file
2. Run `podman image trust show`
3. See it fail with error message regarding missing /etc/containers/policy.json


### Describe the results you received

I get an `Error: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory`.

```bash
$ podman image trust set -t reject default
Error: open /etc/containers/policy.json: permission denied
```

If we enforce with `sudo` then /etc/containers/policy.json gets created.

### Describe the results you expected

I expect the local `policy.json` to be sufficient and be the only file read.

comments.body: Just in case, I discussed this issue with @lsm5 and @vrothberg on the Podman Matrix room where @vrothberg confirmed that it seemed unexpected.

Hi!

Just had a look at this and seems like the problem lies here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9d9f4aaafea01b6604c3a54b9a934c21090ef64a/pkg/trust/policy.go#L56-L67

I tried using an implementation similar to the one used in the `image` package and seems like this would solve the problem:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9d9f4aaafea01b6604c3a54b9a934c21090ef64a/vendor/github.com/containers/image/v5/signature/policy_config.go#L63-L75

I wouldn't mind contributing a PR for this, however, I still want to hear your thoughts (since maybe this is not the correct way of tackling this, or perhaps you have some plans regarding this task, etc.)

@ariasmn Yes, I think updating `podman/pkg/trust.DefaultPolicyPath` seems reasonable. Conceptually I’m not much of a fan of exposing `signature.defaultPolicyPathWithHomeDir` as a stable API (that commits us to the policy being in a single file, for example) — but then `podman trust` really needs that value, so we ended up with a copy in `podman/pkg/trust`.

Nothing about `userPolicyFile` is configurable, and I don’t think it makes any sense to make that an option. Similarly to the exiting code in `policy_config.go`, I would recommend just hard-coding a value in `trust/policy.go`.

Just created a PR.

Please let me know if any further work is needed on my part!
Nice, thank you very much, will try this as soon as possible.
